title: spex-brick-mesh (one resolved LDraw part, cached)
description: Written/read by unibrick/brickmesh.py. A single real LDraw part (https://ldraw.org), resolved once — network fetch + recursive part/subpart/primitive reference-tree walk, both real — into a flat triangle list, in the part's own local, untransformed LDraw frame (LDU units, matrix=identity, translation=zero). Cached under unibrick/.ldraw-cache/meshes/ so regenerating the same part at a different color or point density never re-walks the LDraw reference tree again. Not produced by spex-cli/Rust — generated and consumed entirely by the unibrick/ Python scripts, so unlike this project's other spec'd formats it isn't (yet) checked by crates/spex-cli/tests/schema_validation.rs; see unibrick/brickmesh.py's validate_mesh() for the hand-rolled structural check actually run against real output.

sourcePart.description: The real LDraw part filename this mesh was resolved from (e.g. "3005.dat", a real 1x1 brick), relative to LDraw's official parts/ folder.

sourceDescription.description: The real part's own descriptive title, from its LDraw file's first `0 <description>` line (e.g. "Brick  1 x  1") — null if that line was missing or unparseable.

requestedColorCode.description: The real LDraw color code this part was resolved with (see LDConfig.ldr) — every triangle whose own color was LDraw color 16 ("inherit") got substituted with this code during resolution. brickmesh.place_mesh() uses this to approximate recoloring without re-resolving: triangles matching this code are remapped, genuinely fixed-color triangles are left alone.

units.description: LDraw Units — 1 LDU = 0.4mm, see BRICKs.md. Not millimeters; callers convert at the point they need real-world units (see unibrick/ldraw.py's LDU_TO_MM).

attribution.description: Human-readable real licensing attribution for the LDraw Parts Library (CC BY 2.0 / CCAL 2.0) — carried in the file itself so a mesh is self-describing if it outlives the script that made it.

colorCode.description: This triangle's real, already-resolved LDraw color code (never 16 — "inherit" was substituted with the requesting color during resolution, see requestedColorCode).
